doctor_turn: Hello, I’m Dr. Lee. What brought you in today?
patient_turn: Hi, um, I’ve been having some pretty bad shortness of breath for the last four days. It started off, you know, kinda mild, but then it got worse. I also have these chills and, um, sharp chest pain that feels worse when I take a deep breath.

doctor_turn: When did the shortness of breath and chest pain first start?
patient_turn: So, the shortness of breath started about four days ago, and, um, the chest pain came on a bit later, like, I think it was around the same time as the chills, which was three days ago. It’s been really uncomfortable, to be honest.

doctor_turn: Can you point to where the chest pain is located? Does it spread anywhere else?
patient_turn: Yeah, um, the chest pain is right in the central part of my chest. It’s sharp and pleuritic, but it doesn’t really spread anywhere else, you know? It just hurts more when I take a deep breath or, um, when I move certain ways.

doctor_turn: What does the chest pain feel like? Is it sharp, dull, or something else?
patient_turn: It’s definitely sharp, like, um, a stabbing kind of pain. It really gets worse with deep breaths, but it feels a bit better when I sit forward or, um, when I breathe out. It’s just, honestly, really uncomfortable.

doctor_turn: What were you doing when the chest pain first started?
patient_turn: Um... I was just , I think.

doctor_turn: When did you first notice the chest pain?

doctor_turn: How has the pain changed since it first started?
patient_turn: Well, it’s, um, gotten more intense since it first started. At first, it was just a little annoying, but now it’s really sharp and, um, it hurts a lot more when I breathe deeply. I’m kinda worried about it, to be honest.

doctor_turn: Can you tell me exactly where the pain is located?
patient_turn: It's... in the area.

doctor_turn: How would you describe the pain? Is it sharp, dull, or something else?
patient_turn: It’s sharp, definitely sharp. Like, um, a stabbing pain that really gets worse with deep breaths. It’s pretty uncomfortable, to be honest.

doctor_turn: What makes the pain better or worse for you?
patient_turn: Um, it gets worse when I take a deep breath or, like, when I move certain ways. But it feels a bit better when I sit forward or, um, when I breathe out. So, yeah, it’s a bit of a relief when I do that.

doctor_turn: When did you first notice the chest pain?
patient_turn: I first noticed the chest pain about three days ago, um, after the shortness of breath started getting worse. It just kind of came on suddenly, you know?

doctor_turn: Can you describe where exactly the pain is located?
patient_turn: So, the pain is mostly in the center of my chest, but it’s kind of hard to explain because sometimes it feels like it moves a bit, you know? Also, I’ve been thinking about getting a new couch because the old one is really uncomfortable, especially when I sit for too long. My husband says I should just get a nice recliner instead.

doctor_turn: What does the pain feel like? Is it sharp, dull, or something else?
patient_turn: It’s sharp, definitely sharp. Like, um, a stabbing pain that really gets worse when I take a deep breath. It’s honestly pretty concerning and uncomfortable.

doctor_turn: What makes the pain better or worse for you?
patient_turn: Well, um, it gets worse when I take a deep breath or move around too much. But it feels a bit better when I sit forward or, like, when I breathe out. So, I’ve been trying to find positions that help ease it a little.

doctor_turn: Have you experienced any cough or fever recently?
patient_turn: No, I haven’t had a cough or fever, um, at least not that I’ve measured. I’ve just had this mild, persistent runny nose for about a week, but that’s it. I’m kinda worried that it might be something more serious, though.

doctor_turn: Have you noticed any night sweats or changes in your sleeping patterns?
patient_turn: No, I haven’t had any night sweats or, um, changes in my sleeping patterns. I’ve been sleeping okay, but, you know, the shortness of breath and chest pain have been really distracting. It’s just hard to relax with all of this going on.

doctor_turn: Have you felt dizzy or faint at any time recently?
patient_turn: No, I haven’t felt dizzy or faint at all. Um, I’m just really focused on the shortness of breath and the chest pain, you know? It’s been a bit overwhelming.

patient_turn: No, I haven’t noticed any swelling in my legs or ankles. It’s just been the shortness of breath and the chest pain that’s really bothering me. I’m, um, really hoping to figure out what’s going on soon.

doctor_turn: Have you experienced any palpitations or a racing heart lately?
patient_turn: No, I haven’t had any palpitations or a racing heart. It’s mostly just the shortness of breath and the chest pain that’s been, um, really concerning for me. I just want to get to the bottom of this.

doctor_turn: Do you ever feel short of breath at night?

doctor_turn: What past medical conditions have you been diagnosed with?

doctor_turn: When did you quit smoking, and how much did you smoke before that?
patient_turn: I quit smoking about 20 years ago after smoking for, um, 25 years at one pack per day. It was a long time ago, but I still think about it sometimes, you know?

patient_turn: No, I don’t use any recreational drugs. I’ve just been, um, focused on managing my health and, you know, dealing with everything that’s been going on lately.
